The greatest integer in a real number \(x\), denoted by \([x]\), is the largest integer less than or equal to \(x\). Examples: \([5/2]=2\), \([-5/2]=-3\), \([2]=2\).

The greatest integer function is also known as the floor function, with notation \(\lfloor x \rfloor\).

The ceiling function of a real number \(x\), denoted by \(\lceil x \rceil\), is the smallest integer greater than or equal to \(x\).

  • Examples: \(\lceil 5/2 \rceil = 3\).
Prove that:$$ n \in \mathb{Z} \implies \lfloor x + n \rfloor = \lfloor x \rfloor + n $$
What is the value of \(\lfloor x \rfloor + \lfloor -x \rfloor\) when \(x\) is a real number?
0 if \(x\) is an integer and -1 otherwise.
Prove that \([x + 1/2]\) is the integer nearest to \(x\).
